items.title: Uber, Wayve and Waymo are headed towards a robotaxi showdown in London
items.summary: Uber customers in the UK can now join an interest list to increase their chances of being matched with a Wayve robotaxi.

items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
items.summary: We're likely to see more price increases as the big AI companies plan to go public.

items.title: Notion restores access to Anthropic after service disruption
items.summary: Notion's head of product said he was "astonished" at “the amount of people RT-ing this."

items.title: OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
items.summary: "Chat is dead" — at least, according to a senior OpenAI employee.

items.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Inside GM’s $900M EV battery gamble
items.summary: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

items.title: Hacked, leaked, and held for ransom: the worst breaches of 2026 so far
items.summary: From a massive DOGE data breach and the hacking of critical energy and water systems to the hack of an FBI surveillance system, here are the most damaging security incidents and data breaches of 2026.

items.title: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
items.summary: Even with Lockdown Mode, ChatGPT could be still vulnerable to prompt injections, but the goal is to reduce the likelihood that sensitive data gets shared in the process.

items.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
items.summary: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

items.title: Sriram Krishnan is leaving his role as White House AI advisor
items.summary: Krishnan is reportedly starting a new institution to continue shaping Trump's AI policy.

items.title: The Trump administration might take an equity stake in OpenAI
items.summary: President Donald Trump said he's discussing deals "where the American people can benefit from the success of AI."

items.title: Beyond Instagram: Introducing the next generation of social apps
items.summary: These newer social apps offer alternatives to Big Tech’s feeds, focusing on interests, creativity, and community.

items.title: Reid Hoffman is leaving Microsoft’s board to go ‘founder mode’ with  startup Manus
items.summary: After a very profitable decade on Microsoft's board, Reid Hoffman is stepping down to focus on his AI drug discovery startup Manus.

items.title: Founders share VC horror stories, and some are naming names
items.summary: A massive viral conversation sharing VC horror stories has taken place this week on X. Some are weird. Some are infuriating.

items.title: Former cyber executive turned whistleblower accuses IBM of covering up several data breaches
items.summary: IBM and two of its subsidiary companies were allegedly breached during the mid-2010s — a lawsuit filed by a former cybersecurity executive accuses IBM of not disclosing and actively covering it up.

items.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications officially close in 3 days
items.summary: Applications for Startup Battlefield 200 officially close on June 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Don't wait any longer. Secure your shot at competing on the Disrupt Stage at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 this October at San Francisco's Moscone West.

items.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
items.summary: In a statement, a Google representative described the deal as a result of unexpected demand for its recently launched AI products.

items.title: GM’s electric future depends on a new battery — and this facility
items.summary: GM wants to slash EV prices by deploying new battery tech up to a year earlier than planned. This building is key to making that happen.

items.title: As VC-backed e-bike startups went bankrupt, bootstrapped Lectric grew
items.summary: Lectric, which says the U.S. market is ripe for competition and choice, has launched three new brands in the past six months.

items.title: The most interesting startups right now want to get you off your phone
items.summary: While the AI fundraising machine keeps breaking its own records, some founders are building in the other direction.  Mirror founder Brynn Putnam just raised money for Board, a startup focused on bringing people together through in-person games and social experiences. Cyberdeck creators are going viral crafting whimsical DIY computers that literally encourage users to touch grass. Unlike the AI-free browser crowd, this doesn’t just feel like backlash, […]

items.title: Supabase doubles valuation to $10B in 8 months
items.summary: Supabase, an example of an open source project becoming a fast-growing company, has greatly benefited from AI tools like Claude, Codex, and other vibe-coding platforms.
